# Timegrapher.ai

> Free browser-based mechanical watch timegrapher. Measures rate, beat error, amplitude, and BPH using microphone input. Includes AI-powered diagnosis.

Timegrapher.ai is a single-page web application that functions as a digital timegrapher for mechanical watches. It requires no installation and no account — users allow microphone access in their browser and hold or place their watch near the microphone to receive measurements.

## What it measures

- **Rate (seconds per day)**: How many seconds per day the watch gains (+) or loses (−). COSC chronometer standard is −4 to +6 s/d. ±15 s/d is acceptable for everyday wear.
- **Beat error (milliseconds)**: The timing difference between the tick and tock of the escapement. Under 0.5 ms is excellent; over 2.5 ms can cause the watch to stop.
- **Amplitude (degrees)**: The arc through which the balance wheel swings on each beat. 220–310° is healthy; below 200° suggests a mainspring or servicing issue.
- **BPH (beats per hour)**: The frequency of the movement. Common values: 18,000 (5 Hz), 21,600 (6 Hz), 28,800 (8 Hz), 36,000 (10 Hz).

## How it works

1. The browser captures microphone audio at 44,100 Hz via the Web Audio API.
2. A high-pass filter removes low-frequency noise while preserving tick transients.
3. A peak-follower envelope detector identifies beat events.
4. Rate, beat error, amplitude, and BPH are derived using the same algorithm as the open-source tg-timer project by Marcello Mamino.
5. Waveforms (tic, toc, and full period) are accumulated and displayed on canvas elements.
6. A scrolling paperstrip shows the historical trace of beat timing.

## AI diagnosis

Users can tap "Diagnose my watch" to send their current readings to a secure AI backend. The AI provides:
- A plain-English verdict on the watch's condition
- Likely causes of any out-of-range readings
- Actionable recommendations (noting what is safe DIY vs needs a watchmaker)

The AI only discusses mechanical watches and horology. All guardrails are enforced server-side.

## Technical notes

- No data is stored or transmitted except AI diagnosis requests (which go to a Cloudflare Worker proxy).
- The app works offline once loaded (except for the AI feature).
- Requires: Chrome 80+, Firefox 76+, or Safari 14.5+.
- A dedicated contact microphone improves signal quality significantly over a built-in laptop mic.

## Optional links

- App: https://timegrapher.ai/
- Sitemap: https://timegrapher.ai/sitemap.xml
